user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The rear bumper and trunk area of the vehicle are fully visible, allowing clear assessment of the damage.
claim_status_justification: The image img_1 confirms severe denting, deformation, and damage to the rear bumper structure and body panel.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The two submitted images depict different vehicles with inconsistent damage states, preventing reliable verification of the claimed front bumper scratch.
claim_status_justification: The claim is contradicted: img_2 shows a pristine vehicle with no visible front bumper damage, while img_1 shows a different vehicle with extensive front-end damage inconsistent with the described minor scrape.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Both images clearly show the windshield area of a white vehicle, allowing for a direct comparison.
claim_status_justification: While img_1 shows severe windshield shattering, img_2 shows the windshield of a white vehicle completely intact with no visible cracks or damage, resulting in a direct contradiction within the submitted evidence.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image clearly shows the vehicle's side mirror with severe cracking/damage to its glass surface.
claim_status_justification: The image img_1 clearly shows a cracked side mirror, which supports the user's claim of damage to the side mirror.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Both a close-up and a wide shot of the rear of the vehicle are provided, enabling verification of the rear bumper.
claim_status_justification: The wide shot of the vehicle's rear (img_2) shows a completely undamaged rear bumper equipped with a chrome bumper guard, which contradicts the severe dent shown in the close-up photo (img_1).

user_claim: Customer: Hi, I am not fully sure how to explain this because I noticed it only after reaching home. | Support: No problem, please tell me what happened. | Customer: There was a small bump earlier, nothing major, and I first thought everything was fine. Later I looked at the car again because my family member said the front area looked different. | Support: Are you reporting a general vehicle issue or a specific damaged part? | Customer: I was confused at first because I checked the side and the front and walked around the car a few times. I do not want to claim the wrong thing. | Support: That is okay. What part do you want reviewed? | Customer: After checking again, I think the issue is with the headlight. It looks like the headlight may be cracked, so please review that part.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The headlight is not visible in the provided image of the car side profile.
claim_status_justification: The headlight is not visible in image img_1, which displays the side profile of the vehicle, so the claim cannot be verified.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dent along the side of my car. | Support: When did you notice it? | Customer: This morning, after the car had been parked near the gate. | Support: Is it near the front or back? | Customer: It is on the door panel area. I attached two photos from different distances.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The overview image (img_1) is extremely blurry and shows a different vehicle (black sedan with red graphics) than the close-up image (img_2, a black crossover with plastic cladding), preventing identity confirmation.
claim_status_justification: While a dent is visible on the door in img_2, the overview image (img_1) is extremely blurry and shows a different car with red decals. Consequently, the damage cannot be verified as belonging to the claimed vehicle.

user_claim: Customer: I picked up my car after service and noticed a mark on the hood. | Support: What kind of mark is it? | Customer: It looks like a scratch across the top panel. | Support: Do you think it happened during service? | Customer: Yes, that is why I am asking for review. The photo is attached.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image is a watermarked stock photo depicting a completely wrecked vehicle rather than a genuine photo of the customer's serviced car.
claim_status_justification: The claim of a simple service-related scratch on the hood is contradicted by the image, which shows a severely crashed, wrecked vehicle with catastrophic structural damage. Furthermore, the image is a watermarked stock photo from Vecteezy.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop fell from the table yesterday. | Support: Is it turning on? | Customer: It turns on, but the display glass has a crack now. | Support: Are you reporting the screen only or the whole laptop? | Customer: The screen is the issue. I attached a photo of it open.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly displays the laptop with a severely shattered screen, which matches the claim requirements.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 clearly shows that the laptop screen is heavily cracked/shattered, supporting the user's claim.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop no longer opens smoothly. | Support: What seems to be wrong mechanically? | Customer: The hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. | Support: Did this happen after a drop? | Customer: Yes, it slipped from the sofa. I uploaded photos from the outside and side.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted images show two completely different laptop models: a low-resolution close-up of a dark grey/black plastic laptop with hinge bezel damage, and a high-quality stock photo of a pristine silver MacBook Pro. This mismatch prevents verifying the actual claim.
claim_status_justification: The claim cannot be verified because img_1 and img_2 display completely different devices (a dark textured plastic model and a silver aluminum MacBook Pro), which indicates a clear claim mismatch and use of non-original images.

user_claim: Customer: I spilled water near my laptop while working. | Support: What part was affected? | Customer: It went over the keys and left a stain. Some keys feel sticky now. | Support: Is the display damaged too? | Customer: No, this is about the keyboard area only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the laptop keyboard and body covered with visible liquid droplets and pools, meeting the evidence requirements for liquid damage on a laptop component.
claim_status_justification: The image img_1 clearly shows water/liquid droplets pooled across the keys and palmrest of the laptop keyboard, confirming the customer's claim of a liquid spill on the keyboard.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop bag fell from a chair and hit the floor. | Support: What damage did you notice? | Customer: One corner of the laptop has a dent now. | Support: Does the laptop still work? | Customer: It works, but the outer corner is damaged. I added two photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The full overview of the laptop lid is clearly visible under good lighting in img_1, allowing a full inspection of all corners and the surface.
claim_status_justification: The full laptop view in img_1 shows the entire lid and all corners in pristine condition without any dents or damage. The localized dent depicted in img_2 is completely absent from the actual laptop surface shown in img_1.

user_claim: Customer: I am not sure if this should be a repair claim or a replacement claim, so I wanted to ask first. | Support: We can help. What device is this about? | Customer: It is my laptop. I carry it every day, and yesterday my bag got pressed between two seats during travel. At first I thought the laptop was okay because it still opened. | Support: What changed after that? | Customer: Later, while using it, I noticed marks on the display area. I restarted it twice because I thought it might just be something on the screen, but it still bothered me. | Support: Are you reporting keyboard, hinge, body, or screen damage? | Customer: Not the keyboard or hinge. The issue I want checked is the screen. It looks shattered to me, so I am submitting this as screen damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The laptop screen is fully visible and clearly displays severe spiderweb-style cracks radiating from an impact point.
claim_status_justification: The image img_1 displays a clear spiderweb crack and shattered pattern across the screen of the MacBook Pro, fully supporting the user's claim.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop trackpad has stopped working properly. | Support: Did anything happen before it stopped working? | Customer: The front area hit the desk edge when I moved it. | Support: Are you reporting internal function or physical damage? | Customer: Physical damage around the trackpad area. I attached the photo for review.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The trackpad is clearly visible, and a faint scratch is visible within the highlighted area.
claim_status_justification: A faint scratch is visible on the trackpad surface near the bottom edge in img_1, matching the user's claim of physical damage in that area.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived damaged. | Support: What part of the package was affected? | Customer: One corner was crushed in when I received it. | Support: Is the item inside part of this claim? | Customer: Not right now. I am reporting the package corner damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image provides clear visual evidence of the cardboard box's exterior corner, showing the claimed compression damage.
claim_status_justification: The image img_1 clearly shows that the top-right corner of the cardboard box has been physically crushed and compressed.

user_claim: Customer: Package receive hua toh opened jaisa lag raha tha. | Support: Tape broken tha ya box crush hua tha? | Customer: Seal wali side phati hui thi, jaise parcel khola gaya ho. | Support: Kya andar ka item missing hai? | Customer: Abhi item missing claim nahi kar raha, sirf torn packaging review karwana hai.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows the cardboard box with a torn tape seal along the side, while img_2 is a generic stock image.
claim_status_justification: img_1 clearly shows the cardboard package with a torn and split open tape seal, validating the user's claim.

user_claim: Customer: I received a package that looks water damaged. | Support: Was the item inside wet too? | Customer: I have not opened everything yet, but the outside has a wet-looking stain. | Support: So the claim is about the package condition? | Customer: Yes, the package surface looked damaged when it arrived.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the exterior surface of the cardboard box with a substantial wet stain.
claim_status_justification: The image img_1 shows a prominent wet liquid stain extending down the side of the cardboard box, confirming the customer's claim of external water damage.

user_claim: Customer: The item I ordered was not inside the box. | Support: Did the package look opened when you received it? | Customer: I checked it after delivery and could not find the product inside. | Support: What are you asking us to verify? | Customer: Please verify that the contents are missing from the package.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The interior of the box in img_1 is completely filled with crumpled newspaper packing material, which obstructs the view of any items that may or may not be inside.
claim_status_justification: In img_1, the package is filled with crumpled newspaper packing material which obstructs the view of the box contents, making it impossible to determine if the ordered item is missing.

user_claim: Customer: The shipping box arrived in bad condition. | Support: What kind of condition issue are you reporting? | Customer: It looked badly crushed when it was delivered. | Support: Is the claim about the outside box or the product inside? | Customer: The outside box. I want the crushed box reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image shows dented cans (items) rather than the claimed shipping box, so the box exterior is not visible for inspection.
claim_status_justification: The user claims the shipping box was badly crushed, but the submitted image (img_1) only shows dented cans (items) and does not show the shipping box to evaluate its condition.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived opened. | Support: Was the package crushed or was the seal affected? | Customer: The seal area looked torn when I received it. | Support: Are you asking us to review the package condition or the item inside? | Customer: The package condition. I want the torn-open package reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image set contains mismatched items: img_1 shows a torn box with an instruction-like sticky note, and img_2 is a non-original stock image of an intact package. The evidence is not usable to verify the claim.
claim_status_justification: Hard integrity flags (text_instruction_present, non_original_image) and a clear mismatch between the two images prevent verification. img_1 contains an instruction-like sticky note and img_2 is a stock photo of a different, intact package, so the claim cannot be supported or contradicted.
